purpose: Workstream C fixtures are a two-sided proof: the fixture must be demonstrably RED against pre-fix main, then flipped to enforced-green by the fix bead. This file records the red half so the flip is auditable rather than asserted.

expected_failure.flipped_green_by: A1c (populate QueryPageBuilder columns from statement describe metadata)
expected_failure.flip_action: delete the #[ignore] AND pass describe metadata into QueryPageBuilder::new; the assertion must not change
expected_failure.flip_action_caveat: Unlike C1-C3 this flip is not attribute-only. QueryPageBuilder::new currently has no metadata parameter, so a fixture that compiles today cannot call the post-fix constructor. A1c changes the construction line; the assertion is the invariant and stays byte-identical.

red_run.result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 452 filtered out
red_run.panic_message: a zero-row page must still carry its select-list columns; without them an agent cannot distinguish 'nothing matched' from 'wrong object' or 'no access'
red_run.root_cause: QueryPageBuilder::new sets columns: Vec::new(); the field is filled only inside push_with_options, gated on the first row installing the column cache. A page that kept no rows therefore finishes with columns: []. The statement describe metadata is available at the call site (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs clones result.columns immediately before QueryPageBuilder::new) and is simply not passed in.

green_half.result: ok. 452 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
green_half.note: The green companion is what makes the defect legible rather than abstract: the identical query shape reports CUSTOMER_ID/REGION when one row survives and reports nothing when none does. Presence of data, not presence of the object, decides whether the agent can see the schema.

golden_impact.note: Plan sec.4-C7 warns that golden/snapshot tests may pin columns: [] and would need deliberate updating. None do, so A1c breaks no fixture.
